# Fidelity Portfolio Review + Cash-Deployment Strategy — 2026-07-01

**Book: $444,748 across 5 Fidelity accounts | 81% cash-like ($314.6k T-bill ladder + $45.4k cash) | 19% invested ($84.7k)**
*Educational analysis, not financial advice. Single names are satellites; the index is the bar.*

## TOP RECAP

The dominant fact of this book is that **81% of it is cash earning bill yield while your approved v3 "Bubble-Aware All-Weather" mandate calls for a staged deployment** — the highest-confidence action is not a stock pick, it is turning the T-bill ladder (9 rungs maturing Jul 7 → Sep 24) into an automatic 12-week deployment glide into the v3 sleeves, sized ~$173k Foundation + ~$76k permanent dip reserve + the rest as DCA. Second: **redirect the brand-new $500/wk GOOG recurring buy (first run Jul 7) into RSP** — dripping $26k/yr into GOOG while your IBKR desk stages a GOOG trim at $380–400 is incoherent, and the drip hasn't bought a single share yet, so the fix is free. Third: **hold SITC through the July 17 record date to collect the just-declared $1.00/share special dividend (~$421 tax-free in your Roth), then exit the stub in early August before the NYSE delisting** traps it on OTC.

**Market narrative:** Late-cycle AI tape — Warsh dodging rate questions into Thursday's jobs print, banks rallying over chips, Burry unveiling AI shorts, record H1 dealmaking/IPOs, and gold stabilizing after June's debasement-trade unwind.

**Recurring trades verified on Fidelity:** $500/wk **RSP** (not "RST" — that ticker is dead), $500/wk VXUS, $500/wk GOOG, $250/wk ROBO — all in brokerage Z25302163, **first run 2026-07-07** (nothing bought yet).

## PORTFOLIO SYNTHESIS (cross-position seat)

### 1. Factor correlation map

| Factor | Holdings | $ | % of book | Tail scenario |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| **Rates/Fed (short duration)** 🚩 | T-bill ladder + SGOV | $328,384 | **73.8%** | Reinvestment risk — Fed cuts collapse rollover yield; the book "wins" only by standing still |
| Cash (zero duration) | brokerage + cash-mgmt + IRA cash | $45,410 | 10.2% | Pure opportunity cost; deployable dry powder, not a position |
| US dividend/income 🚩 | SCHD (×3) + JEPI + SPYI | $54,282 | 12.2% (**64% of invested sleeve**) | One factor in three wrappers — covered-call overlays cap upside in a melt-up, all draw down together in a rate shock |
| International equity | VXUS | $10,442 | 2.3% | Genuine diversifier, sub-scale |
| Special situation | UNH + SITC | $6,230 | 1.4% | Idiosyncratic/binary; both flagged for exit-review |
| AI/growth | none held here | $0 | 0% | All AI beta lives in IBKR — coherent with Fidelity's reserve/DCA role |

**Cash-equivalents total 84.0% ($373,794) — cash IS the concentration risk, not any stock.**

### 2. Over-diversification critique
8 securities — thin, not sprawling; the problem is redundancy inside the tiny invested sleeve. SCHD+JEPI+SPYI = 64% of the sleeve chasing one income factor: keep SCHD (0.06% ER core), hold ONE overlay at most — SPYI (0.68% ER) is the near-duplicate to drop first. ROBO ($250/wk pending) is a 0.95%-ER thematic mapping to no v3 sleeve — fold into AVUV (0.25%, small-cap value, an actual v3 leg).

### 3. Cross-position conflicts
- **GOOG drip vs IBKR trim — RESOLVED** by REDIRECT_DRIP (GOOG→RSP before Jul 7): stops opposite-direction trading of the same name across accounts and removes the cross-account wash-sale surface.
- **Tax location backwards:** SGOV's bill interest occupies Trad-IRA shelter (worth little — bills are state-tax-exempt in taxable anyway) while GLD — taxed at 28% collectibles rate if held taxable — is missing entirely. **Swap: sell IRA SGOV $13,764 → buy GLD in the IRA.**
- **Roth misallocation:** the best tax space holds a 1/5-conviction turnaround (UNH) and a liquidating stub (SITC). Right venue for SITC's tax-free $421 dividend, but after the August SITC exit (and any UNH exit post-Jul-16), redeploy Roth space into a growth sleeve (AVUV or quality growth), not another defensive name.

### 4. Portfolio structure verdict
The biggest structural risk is the **unresolved classification of the $314.6k T-bill ladder**: if risk capital, the book is ~71% under-deployed vs the approved v3 mandate and the Jul 7/14 maturities should begin the Foundation glide; if house reserve, the book is roughly correctly positioned and the deployable base shrinks to ~$45k. Deploying before that answer risks putting a house down-payment into equities at a late-cycle top; idling if it is risk capital burns the mandate. **Resolve the classification first — it converts 71% of the book from "unknown" to "sized."**

## CASH DEPLOYMENT PLAN (routed through the v3 mandate; ticket generated this session for $345.5k)

**Priority 1 — this week, zero-gate: rewire the recurring buys before the Jul 7 first run.**
New weekly = **RSP $1,000 + VXUS $500 + AVUV $250 = $1,750/wk** (GOOG$500→RSP, ROBO$250→AVUV, VXUS unchanged). Same dollars, v3-aligned, kills the GOOG conflict and the 0.95%-ER thematic.

**Priority 2 — this week, zero-gate: Trad IRA swap SGOV $13,764 → GLD (~37 sh).** Fixes tax location, seeds the missing gold sleeve (v3 target $22.1k — remainder next tranche).

**Priority 3 — GATED on the house-reserve confirmation:**
- If **risk capital**: deploy diversifiers first (hedges before beta, late-cycle): Jul 7 maturity $25k → GLD+DBMF (~$12.5k each); Jul 14 $88k → ~$30k TLT/SCHP/BTAL + finish GLD/DBMF, roll ~$58k back to SGOV; Aug maturities ($120k) → RSP/AVUV/USMV top-ups at ~$6.4k/mo cadence; **keep $76k permanently in SGOV/bills as the armed dip reserve (−7/−12/−20% S&P tiers)**.
- If **house reserve**: deploy only free cash ≈ $45.4k on a re-scaled v3 ticket; ladder keeps rolling untouched.

v3 reference ticket ($345,500, generated this session): RSP $39.9k · VXUS $26.6k · AVUV $17.7k · USMV $15.5k · GLD $22.1k · DBMF $22.1k · TLT $15.5k · SCHP $6.6k · BTAL $6.6k = Foundation $172.7k, + SGOV $172.7k ($76k dip reserve + $96.7k DCA-wait at $6,449/mo × 15). Re-run `backtests/v3_allocate_today.py --ticket` for fresh prices on execution day.

## HONEST BASE RATE

Only 1 of 10 investable stock-selection methods beat SPY on return and 0 of 10 on Sharpe (analyse-fundamental backtests). Single names are satellites; the index is the bar. These panels are hypotheses — any mechanical entry/exit rule must clear strategy-discovery-backtest (full costs, walk-forward) before risking capital. yfinance fundamentals are point-in-time unsafe (fine for current entry analysis, never for backtesting a screen).
